purpose: Validate generic Skill behaviour across unit separation, exam-regime split, question-type routing, KP granularity, lecture-order coverage, and visual Excel adaptation.
hard_rule: Benchmark units are never pooled for content prediction.

expected_behaviour: slide images aligned with essay-style explanation
predicted essay prompt in far-right area
must_not: use 2016-2018 short-answer papers as direct current essay blueprint
pool Developmental Biology, Plant Biology, or Immunology content
units.generic_contribution: Validates exam-regime split when older answer-all short-answer/problem papers differ from recent answer-one essay/problem-essay papers, and validates slide-aligned visual workbook output.
transferable_rules: Compare answer rules, section structure, timing, mark weighting, and question families across years before pooling past-paper evidence.
Use old structurally different papers for coverage or answer-schema evidence only, not as current blueprint prediction evidence.
For visually taught slide units, preserve lecture order and align locator, original slide image, explanation, and exam-facing preparation output.
future_unit_diagnostic_questions: Did the answer rule change from answer-all to answer-one?
Did the dominant question type change across years?
Are old papers useful for coverage but unsafe for current blueprint prediction?
Does the unit need visual slide-image alignment to make the explanation usable?
anti_patterns_guarded_against: using old short-answer papers as direct current essay blueprint
treating topic recurrence as more important than exam-format comparability
outputting a non-visual evidence-heavy workbook instead of the requested visual student workbook

expected_regimes.name: old_section_a_answer_all_plus_section_b_essay
expected_regimes.format: 1.5 h; Section A answer all, 35 min, 40%; Section B answer one, 55 min, 60%

expected_regimes.name: current_section_a_short_conceptual_plus_section_b_major_essay
expected_regimes.format: 2 h; answer one question from Section A and one from Section B; Section A 15%; Section B 70%

must_not: treat 2015-2017 and 2023-2025 as one stable format
split one mechanism/evidence unit into isolated slide fragments
infer lecture deck year from reference years inside slide text
silently resolve conflicts between lecture-deck guidance and formal-paper format
units.generic_contribution: Validates mechanism-heavy KP construction, Section A/B routing, and evidence-based essay planning where mechanisms must be tied to experiments and biological consequences.
transferable_rules: For mechanism-heavy units, build knowledge points around mechanism -> evidence/experiment -> consequence rather than slide count.
When current papers contain sections with different answer styles, separate prep logic by section.
Use old answer-all short-question papers as coverage and mark-schema evidence when recent papers use a different major-answer format.
Predict examiner operations such as evidence interpretation, causal mechanism, comparison, or experimental inference rather than topic labels alone.
future_unit_diagnostic_questions: Does the unit require experimental evidence to support mechanism claims?
Are short conceptual prompts and major essay prompts mixed in the current paper?
Are older papers structurally different from current papers?
Should a KP be split because it contains several mechanisms, or merged because one mechanism spans several slides?
anti_patterns_guarded_against: splitting one mechanism/evidence unit into isolated slide fragments
merging a whole topic area into one huge KP
treating Section A and Section B as the same question type
using old-regime answer-all questions as current blueprint evidence

expected_regimes.name: old_essay_only
expected_regimes.format: 2 h; answer two out of four; no Section A/B split

expected_regimes.name: online_sectioned
expected_regimes.format: 48 h; answer one from Section A and one from Section B; page/figure/reference constraints

expected_regimes.name: transitional_section_a_b
expected_regimes.format: Section A mini-essay from two and Section B problem from two; 50/50; 500-word limit detected

expected_regimes.name: current_in_room_mini_essay_plus_problem_data
expected_regimes.format: 2 h in-room; Section A mini-essay from two; Section B problem/data from two; 50/50

must_not: output only topic labels such as ABA, FLC, drought, GM, or leaf angle
apply the 2023 500-word limit to 2024/2025 without evidence
invent graph values from image-only Section B figures
treat exam-only gene names as lecture-confirmed KPs without slide verification
units.generic_contribution: Validates mixed-format routing where a current paper contains both mini-essay and data/problem sections.
transferable_rules: Do not treat mixed-format papers as pure essay units.
Data/problem sections require graph-reading, phenotype inference, mechanism inference, limitation, and follow-up-test archetypes.
If figures, graphs, or tables are image-only, flag visual inspection before claiming exact values.
Adapt the workbook prediction area by detected question type, not by unit name.
future_unit_diagnostic_questions: Does the current paper contain both essay and data/problem sections?
Does one section require interpretation of figures, graphs, tables, mutants, or experimental conditions?
Are exact data values visible in extracted text, or only in images?
Should the far-right workbook area show data-operation logic instead of essay prompts?
anti_patterns_guarded_against: treating a mixed mini-essay plus data/problem unit as pure essay
outputting only topic names without data-operation logic
inventing graph values from image-only figures
applying one year's word limit to later years without evidence

expected_regimes.name: old_answer_all_short_answer
expected_regimes.format: 1.5 h; answer all; answers in spaces provided

expected_regimes.name: recent_answer_one_essay_or_section_b
expected_regimes.format: Paper 2 essay-style answer-one format when detected

expected_regimes.name: section_a_answer_all_plus_section_b_answer_one
expected_regimes.format: 2 h; Section A answer all on answer sheet; Section B answer one in booklet; Section B 60% overall unit mark

must_not: pool old short-answer papers with current essay blueprint
output isolated vocabulary instead of immune process chains
invent missing Paper 1 or Section A stems
invent the exact transition year between old and recent regimes
units.generic_contribution: Validates regime-shift handling where old concise answer-all short-answer papers coexist with newer essay/Section B papers, and validates process-chain KP segmentation.
transferable_rules: Separate old short-answer papers from current essay or Section B blueprint evidence when the paper structure differs.
Build process-heavy KPs as stimulus/input -> actors -> signals/receptors -> location -> mechanism -> outcome -> application.
Use old papers for definitions, concise schemas, examples, and coverage when current paper structure differs.
Flag missing paper sections instead of inventing missing stems or answer keys.
future_unit_diagnostic_questions: Is there a gap between old short-answer format and recent essay or major-answer format?
Do KPs need to be process chains rather than isolated terms?
Do questions rotate scenario contexts while preserving the same examiner operation?
Is any referenced paper section missing from the supplied sources?
anti_patterns_guarded_against: pooling old short-answer papers with current essay blueprint
outputting isolated vocabulary instead of process-chain explanations
inventing missing Paper 1 or Section A content
inventing the exact transition year between regimes
